chapters.title: — COLD OPEN: What Changed This Week
— HEADLINE: Effect Size and the Causation Problem
— HEADLINE: Practice Impact and the Prior Coverage Note
— ROUND 1: Keller — Resolved Early FGR and Neonatal Morbidity
— ROUND 2: Hannoun — Anti-HLA Antibodies and FGR
— ROUND 3: Kabiri — Late Short Cervix and Progesterone (Affectionate Disagreement)
— ROUND 4: Ambia — Breakthrough Eclampsia on Magnesium
— CLOSE: What to Watch, What's Still Open

key_claims.claim: Widespread valacyclovir uptake in France after 2020 was associated with substantial reduction in vertical CMV transmission at the national level over 7 years
Fetal growth restriction diagnosed at anatomy scan that subsequently normalizes still carries elevated severe neonatal morbidity compared to consistently normal-growth pregnancies
Elevated maternal anti-HLA antibodies are associated with increased risk of fetal growth restriction, suggesting an immune-mediated mechanism
Vaginal progesterone for short cervix first identified after 24 weeks may not reduce spontaneous preterm birth
Breakthrough eclampsia despite magnesium sulfate prophylaxis occurs and carries significant maternal and perinatal morbidity
